name: Cabrakan
description: Cabrakan, whose name translates to "Earthquake," was a giant and a son of Vucub Caquix and Chimalmat in Mayan mythology. He was known for his immense strength and ability to move mountains with ease. He arrogantly believed he was the one who created the mountains, not the gods. Cabrakan, along with his brother Zipacna, posed a significant threat to the Hero Twins, Hunahpu and Xbalanque, as they were clearing the land for cultivation. Cabrakan was eventually tricked and defeated by the Hero Twins. They poisoned his food with a bird that he loved to eat and, while weakened, he was bound and buried alive in a pit. This act was crucial in the Hero Twins' quest to rid the world of the arrogant and destructive beings who threatened the balance of creation. He represents the destructive force of earthquakes and unchecked pride, serving as a cautionary tale about the dangers of arrogance and disrespect for the divine order. His defeat symbolized the triumph of order over chaos and the importance of humility. Cabrakan's legacy remains as a reminder of the power of the natural world and the consequences of hubris.
subtitle: Earthquake Giant of Mayan Legend

keyMyths.title: The Defeat of Cabrakan
keyMyths.description: The Hero Twins, Hunahpu and Xbalanque, tricked Cabrakan into eating poisoned food. While weakened, they bound and buried him alive, ending his reign of destruction.

keyMyths.title: Cabrakan's Arrogance
keyMyths.description: Cabrakan boasted that he was responsible for creating the mountains, displaying immense arrogance. This angered the Hero Twins and motivated them to defeat him.

keyMyths.title: Family of Destruction
keyMyths.description: Cabrakan, as the son of Vucub Caquix and brother of Zipacna, was part of a family known for their destructive tendencies. They represented a threat to the emerging world order.
